behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real watcher recursion over OS filesystem events (root vs nested paths). A mock watcher can emit a nested path and falsely pass even if real recursive watching is misconfigured. REJECTION-GATE: kept System because behavior text requires actual watched-directory behavior for subdirectories, which is an OS/filesystem-event boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp watched directory, create a nested subdirectory, write a file there, and assert one transformed output is produced without restart.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is local path+time-window state logic; no real OS/network/subprocess needed, and no LLM dependency. MOCK-MISS check: this failure can be caught at one object/function boundary (dedup cache/time comparison). REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text is specifically dedup logic correctness, not watcher/queue handoff correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Call dedup checker twice with identical normalized path inside the configured window using controlled clock/time source; assert first accepted, second rejected; assert acceptance again after window expires.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on OS emitting duplicate filesystem events. In-process fakes can hide backend-specific burst patterns and timing. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both push to System.
behaviors.test_description: On a real filesystem watcher backend, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple events (create+modify/rename sequence) and assert exactly one pipeline execution/output for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: This is OS-level directory lifecycle/inode behavior while process remains live. A mock filesystem watcher may not reproduce remove/recreate semantics, so real infrastructure is required.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real temp directory, delete watched directory, recreate it at same path, add a new file, and assert it is processed without restarting daemon.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff/order logic (arrival queue + timestamp sorter + pipeline dispatch). Can be reproduced with in-process wiring and controlled file metadata/events; real OS behavior is not strictly required to validate ordering rule itself. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS primitive if arrivals are injected, (b)(c)(d) no AI/agentic dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Wire watcher-event adapter, scheduler, and processor together in-process; inject near-simultaneous arrivals with controlled creation timestamps and assert dispatch order follows timestamp order regardless of event arrival order.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real write-in-progress timing and filesystem event behavior (partial writes, close/flush timing). Mocked writes/events can falsely pass and miss race conditions.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on real filesystem, open a file and write in chunks with delays while keeping handle open; assert daemon does not process mid-write and processes once after final flush/close.
